title: Security incident disclosure — July 2026
excerpt: We’re on a journey to advance and democratize artificial intelligence through open source and open science.
content_html:
Back to Articles

  • What happened
  • What we did
  • For our community
  • Analyzing an AI-driven intrusion
    • The asymmetry problem
  • What this means

Earlier this week, we detected and responded to an intrusion into part of our production infrastructure. This one was different from anything we had handled before in one important way: it was driven, end to end, by an autonomous AI agent system - and we detected and dissected it largely with AI of our own.

We identified unauthorized access to a limited set of internal datasets and to several credentials used by our services. We are still completing our assessment of whether any partner or customer data was affected, and we will contact any affected parties directly as required. We have found no evidence of tampering with public, user-facing models, datasets, or Spaces, and our software supply chain (container images and published packages) was verified clean.

What happened

The intrusion started where AI platforms are uniquely exposed: the data-processing pipeline. A malicious dataset abused two code-execution paths in our dataset processing (a remote-code dataset loader and a template-injection in a dataset configuration) to run code on a processing worker. From there, the actor escalated to node-level access, harvested cloud and cluster credentials, and moved laterally into several internal clusters over a weekend.

The campaign was run by an autonomous agent framework (appearing to be built on an agentic security-research harness - used LLM still not known) executing many thousands of individual actions across a swarm of short-lived sandboxes, with self-migrating command-and-control staged on public services. This matches the "agentic attacker" scenario the industry has been forecasting.

What we did

  • Fixed the root vulnerability: the dataset code-execution paths used for initial access are closed.
  • Eradicated the attacker's foothold across the affected clusters and rebuilt the compromised nodes.
  • Revoked and rotated the affected credentials and tokens, and began a broader precautionary rotation of secrets.
  • Deployed additional guardrails and stricter admission controls on our clusters.
  • Improved our detection and alerting so a high-severity signal pages a responder in minutes, any day of the week.

We are working with outside cybersecurity forensic specialists to investigate the issue and review our security policies and procedures. Finally, we have also reported this incident to law enforcement agencies.

For our community

As a precaution, we recommend rotating any access tokens and reviewing recent activity on your account. If you believe you are affected, or want to report a security concern, contact us at security@huggingface.co.

We are grateful to the teams across Hugging Face who responded around the clock, and we are sorry for any disruption this caused. Security is never finished; we will keep raising the bar.

Analyzing an AI-driven intrusion

The attack was initially surfaced through AI-assisted detection. Our anomaly-detection pipeline uses LLM-based triage over security telemetry to separate real signals from the daily noise, and it was the correlation of those signals that flagged the compromise.

To understand what a swarm of tens of thousands of automated actions did, we ran LLM-driven analysis agents over the full attacker action log, comprised of more than 17,000 recorded events. This allowed us to reconstruct the timeline, extract indicators of compromise, map the credentials touched, and separate genuine impact from decoy activity. Thanks to this approach, we were able to do in hours what would usually take days, and match the adversary's speed.

The choice of models we could use for this analysis was constrained in a way we did not anticipate; we describe this below.

The asymmetry problem

When we started the log analysis, we first used frontier models behind commercial APIs. This did not work: the analysis requires submitting large volumes of real attack commands, exploit payloads, and C2 artifacts, and these requests were blocked by the providers' safety guardrails, which cannot distinguish an incident responder from an attacker. We ran the forensic analysis instead on GLM 5.2, an open-weight model, on our own infrastructure. This had a second benefit: no attacker data, and none of the credentials it referenced, left our environment.

This experience points to a gap worth planning for. We do not know which model powered the attacker's agents, whether a jailbroken hosted model or an unrestricted open-weight one; either way, the attacker was bound by no usage policy, while our own forensic work was blocked by the guardrails of the hosted models we first tried. The practical lesson for defenders: have a capable model you can run on your own infrastructure vetted and ready before an incident, both to avoid guardrail lockout and to keep attacker data and credentials from leaving your environment. This is not an argument against safety measures on hosted models, and we are sharing this feedback with the providers concerned.

What this means

Autonomous, AI-driven offensive tooling is no longer theoretical. It lowers the cost of running a broad, patient, multi-stage campaign, and it operates at machine speed. Defending an online platform now means treating the data and model surface as a first-class attack surface, and using AI on defense to keep pace. We will keep investing there, and keep sharing what we learn.

sentences.zh: 本周早些时候，我们检测到对我们部分生产基础设施的入侵并做出了响应。

sentences.en: This one was different from anything we had handled before in one important way: it was driven, end to end, by an autonomous AI agent system - and we detected and dissected it largely with AI of our own.
sentences.zh: 这与我们之前处理过的任何事情都有一个重要的区别：它是由一个自主的人工智能代理系统端到端驱动的——我们主要用我们自己的人工智能来检测和剖析它。

sentences.en: We identified unauthorized access to a limited set of internal datasets and to several credentials used by our services.
sentences.zh: 我们发现对一组有限的内部数据集和我们的服务使用的多个凭据的未经授权的访问。

sentences.en: We are still completing our assessment of whether any partner or customer data was affected, and we will contact any affected parties directly as required.
sentences.zh: 我们仍在完成对是否有任何合作伙伴或客户数据受到影响的评估，我们将根据需要直接联系任何受影响的各方。

sentences.en: We have found no evidence of tampering with public, user-facing models, datasets, or Spaces, and our software supply chain (container images and published packages) was verified clean.
sentences.zh: 我们没有发现任何篡改公共的、面向用户的模型、数据集或空间的证据，并且我们的软件供应链（容器图像和已发布的软件包）已被验证干净。

blocks.html_en: What happened

sentences.en: What happened
sentences.zh: 发生了什么

blocks.html_en: The intrusion started where AI platforms are uniquely exposed: the data-processing pipeline. A malicious dataset abused two code-execution paths in our dataset processing (a remote-code dataset loader and a template-injection in a dataset configuration) to run code on a processing worker. From there, the actor escalated to node-level access, harvested cloud and cluster credentials, and moved laterally into several internal clusters over a weekend.

sentences.en: The intrusion started where AI platforms are uniquely exposed: the data-processing pipeline.
sentences.zh: 入侵始于人工智能平台唯一暴露的地方：数据处理管道。

sentences.en: A malicious dataset abused two code-execution paths in our dataset processing (a remote-code dataset loader and a template-injection in a dataset configuration) to run code on a processing worker.
sentences.zh: 恶意数据集滥用数据集处理中的两个代码执行路径（远程代码数据集加载器和数据集配置中的模板注入）在处理工作线程上运行代码。

sentences.en: From there, the actor escalated to node-level access, harvested cloud and cluster credentials, and moved laterally into several internal clusters over a weekend.
sentences.zh: 从那里，攻击者升级到节点级访问，获取云和集群凭据，并在一个周末横向移动到几个内部集群。

blocks.html_en: The campaign was run by an autonomous agent framework (appearing to be built on an agentic security-research harness - used LLM still not known) executing many thousands of individual actions across a swarm of short-lived sandboxes, with self-migrating command-and-control staged on public services. This matches the "agentic attacker" scenario the industry has been forecasting.

sentences.en: The campaign was run by an autonomous agent framework (appearing to be built on an agentic security-research harness - used LLM still not known) executing many thousands of individual actions across a swarm of short-lived sandboxes, with self-migrating command-and-control staged on public services.
sentences.zh: 该活动由一个自主代理框架（似乎是建立在代理安全研究工具上构建的 - 使用的法学硕士仍然未知）运行，在一群短暂的沙箱中执行数千个单独的操作，并在公共服务上进行自我迁移的命令和控制。

sentences.en: This matches the "agentic attacker" scenario the industry has been forecasting.
sentences.zh: 这符合业界预测的“代理攻击者”场景。

blocks.html_en: What we did

sentences.en: What we did
sentences.zh: 我们做了什么

blocks.html_en: • Fixed the root vulnerability: the dataset code-execution paths used for initial access are closed.

sentences.en: Fixed the root vulnerability: the dataset code-execution paths used for initial access are closed.
sentences.zh: 修复了根漏洞：用于初始访问的数据集代码执行路径被关闭。

blocks.html_en: • Eradicated the attacker's foothold across the affected clusters and rebuilt the compromised nodes.

sentences.en: Eradicated the attacker's foothold across the affected clusters and rebuilt the compromised nodes.
sentences.zh: 根除攻击者在受影响集群中的立足点并重建受感染的节点。

blocks.html_en: • Revoked and rotated the affected credentials and tokens, and began a broader precautionary rotation of secrets.

sentences.en: Revoked and rotated the affected credentials and tokens, and began a broader precautionary rotation of secrets.
sentences.zh: 撤销并轮换受影响的凭证和令牌，并开始更广泛的预防性秘密轮换。

blocks.html_en: • Deployed additional guardrails and stricter admission controls on our clusters.

sentences.en: Deployed additional guardrails and stricter admission controls on our clusters.
sentences.zh: 在我们的集群上部署了额外的护栏和更严格的准入控制。

blocks.html_en: • Improved our detection and alerting so a high-severity signal pages a responder in minutes, any day of the week.

sentences.en: Improved our detection and alerting so a high-severity signal pages a responder in minutes, any day of the week.
sentences.zh: 改进了我们的检测和警报，以便在一周中的任何一天，高强度信号都可以在几分钟内寻呼响应者。

blocks.html_en: We are working with outside cybersecurity forensic specialists to investigate the issue and review our security policies and procedures. Finally, we have also reported this incident to law enforcement agencies.

sentences.en: We are working with outside cybersecurity forensic specialists to investigate the issue and review our security policies and procedures.
sentences.zh: 我们正在与外部网络安全取证专家合作调查该问题并审查我们的安全政策和程序。

sentences.en: Finally, we have also reported this incident to law enforcement agencies.
sentences.zh: 最后，我们也向执法部门举报了这一事件。

blocks.html_en: For our community

sentences.en: For our community
sentences.zh: 对于我们的社区

blocks.html_en: As a precaution, we recommend rotating any access tokens and reviewing recent activity on your account. If you believe you are affected, or want to report a security concern, contact us at security@huggingface.co.

sentences.en: As a precaution, we recommend rotating any access tokens and reviewing recent activity on your account.
sentences.zh: 作为预防措施，我们建议轮换所有访问令牌并检查您帐户上的近期活动。

sentences.en: If you believe you are affected, or want to report a security concern, contact us at security@huggingface.co.
sentences.zh: 如果您认为自己受到了影响，或者想要报告安全问题，请通过 security@huggingface.co 联系我们。

blocks.html_en: We are grateful to the teams across Hugging Face who responded around the clock, and we are sorry for any disruption this caused. Security is never finished; we will keep raising the bar.

sentences.en: We are grateful to the teams across Hugging Face who responded around the clock, and we are sorry for any disruption this caused.
sentences.zh: 我们感谢 Hugging Face 全天候响应的团队，对于由此造成的任何干扰，我们深表歉意。

sentences.en: Security is never finished; we will keep raising the bar.
sentences.zh: 安全永无止境；我们将继续提高标准。

sentences.en: Our anomaly-detection pipeline uses LLM-based triage over security telemetry to separate real signals from the daily noise, and it was the correlation of those signals that flagged the compromise.
sentences.zh: 我们的异常检测管道使用基于 LLM 的安全遥测分类来将真实信号与日常噪声分开，正是这些信号的相关性标记了妥协。

blocks.html_en: To understand what a swarm of tens of thousands of automated actions did, we ran LLM-driven analysis agents over the full attacker action log, comprised of more than 17,000 recorded events. This allowed us to reconstruct the timeline, extract indicators of compromise, map the credentials touched, and separate genuine impact from decoy activity. Thanks to this approach, we were able to do in hours what would usually take days, and match the adversary's speed.

sentences.en: To understand what a swarm of tens of thousands of automated actions did, we ran LLM-driven analysis agents over the full attacker action log, comprised of more than 17,000 recorded events.
sentences.zh: 为了了解数以万计的自动化操作群做了什么，我们在完整的攻击者操作日志上运行了 LLM 驱动的分析代理，该日志由 17,000 多个记录的事件组成。

sentences.en: This allowed us to reconstruct the timeline, extract indicators of compromise, map the credentials touched, and separate genuine impact from decoy activity.
sentences.zh: 这使我们能够重建时间线，提取妥协指标，映射所涉及的凭证，并将真正的影响与诱饵活动区分开来。

sentences.en: Thanks to this approach, we were able to do in hours what would usually take days, and match the adversary's speed.
sentences.zh: 借助这种方法，我们能够在数小时内完成通常需要数天才能完成的任务，并与对手的速度相匹配。

sentences.zh: 我们可以用于此分析的模型选择受到了我们没有预料到的限制；我们在下面对此进行描述。

sentences.en: We ran the forensic analysis instead on GLM 5.2, an open-weight model, on our own infrastructure.
sentences.zh: 我们在 GLM 5.2（一种开放权重模型）上在我们自己的基础设施上运行了取证分析。

sentences.en: This had a second benefit: no attacker data, and none of the credentials it referenced, left our environment.
sentences.zh: 这还有第二个好处：没有攻击者数据，也没有它引用的凭据离开我们的环境。

blocks.html_en: This experience points to a gap worth planning for. This is not an argument against safety measures on hosted models, and we are sharing this feedback with the providers concerned.

sentences.en: This experience points to a gap worth planning for.
sentences.zh: 这一经验指出了一个值得规划的差距。

sentences.en: We do not know which model powered the attacker's agents, whether a jailbroken hosted model or an unrestricted open-weight one; either way, the attacker was bound by no usage policy, while our own forensic work was blocked by the guardrails of the hosted models we first tried.
sentences.zh: 我们不知道攻击者的代理使用哪种模型，是越狱的托管模型还是不受限制的开放重量模型；无论哪种方式，攻击者都不受使用策略的约束，而我们自己的取证工作则受到我们第一次尝试的托管模型的护栏的阻碍。

sentences.en: The practical lesson for defenders: have a capable model you can run on your own infrastructure vetted and ready before an incident, both to avoid guardrail lockout and to keep attacker data and credentials from leaving your environment.
sentences.zh: 防御者的实践教训是：拥有一个可以在事件发生之前在自己的基础设施上运行的强大模型，并进行审查和准备，既可以避免护栏锁定，又可以防止攻击者数据和凭据离开您的环境。

sentences.en: This is not an argument against safety measures on hosted models, and we are sharing this feedback with the providers concerned.
sentences.zh: 这并不是反对托管模型的安全措施，我们正在与相关提供商分享此反馈。

blocks.html_en: What this means

sentences.en: What this means
sentences.zh: 这意味着什么

blocks.html_en: Autonomous, AI-driven offensive tooling is no longer theoretical. It lowers the cost of running a broad, patient, multi-stage campaign, and it operates at machine speed. sentences.zh: 自主的、人工智能驱动的进攻工具不再是理论。

sentences.zh: 它降低了开展广泛、耐心、多阶段活动的成本，并且以机器速度运行。

sentences.en: Defending an online platform now means treating the data and model surface as a first-class attack surface, and using AI on defense to keep pace.
sentences.zh: 现在，防御在线平台意味着将数据和模型表面视为一流的攻击表面，并在防御中使用人工智能来跟上步伐。

sentences.zh: 我们将继续在那里投资，并继续分享我们学到的东西。

content_html_zh:
返回文章

  • 发生了什么
  • 我们做了什么
  • 对于我们的社区
  • 分析人工智能驱动的入侵 不对称问题
  • 这意味着什么

本周早些时候，我们检测到对我们部分生产基础设施的入侵并做出了响应。 这与我们之前处理过的任何事情都有一个重要的区别：它是由一个自主的人工智能代理系统端到端驱动的——我们主要用我们自己的人工智能来检测和剖析它。

我们发现对一组有限的内部数据集和我们的服务使用的多个凭据的未经授权的访问。 我们仍在完成对是否有任何合作伙伴或客户数据受到影响的评估，我们将根据需要直接联系任何受影响的各方。 我们没有发现任何篡改公共的、面向用户的模型、数据集或空间的证据，并且我们的软件供应链（容器图像和已发布的软件包）已被验证干净。

发生了什么

入侵始于人工智能平台唯一暴露的地方：数据处理管道。 恶意数据集滥用数据集处理中的两个代码执行路径（远程代码数据集加载器和数据集配置中的模板注入）在处理工作线程上运行代码。 从那里，攻击者升级到节点级访问，获取云和集群凭据，并在一个周末横向移动到几个内部集群。

该活动由一个自主代理框架（似乎是建立在代理安全研究工具上构建的 - 使用的法学硕士仍然未知）运行，在一群短暂的沙箱中执行数千个单独的操作，并在公共服务上进行自我迁移的命令和控制。 这符合业界预测的“代理攻击者”场景。

我们做了什么

  • 修复了根漏洞：用于初始访问的数据集代码执行路径被关闭。
  • 根除攻击者在受影响集群中的立足点并重建受感染的节点。
  • 撤销并轮换受影响的凭证和令牌，并开始更广泛的预防性秘密轮换。
  • 在我们的集群上部署了额外的护栏和更严格的准入控制。
  • 改进了我们的检测和警报，以便在一周中的任何一天，高强度信号都可以在几分钟内寻呼响应者。

我们正在与外部网络安全取证专家合作调查该问题并审查我们的安全政策和程序。 最后，我们也向执法部门举报了这一事件。

对于我们的社区

作为预防措施，我们建议轮换所有访问令牌并检查您帐户上的近期活动。 如果您认为自己受到了影响，或者想要报告安全问题，请通过 security@huggingface.co 联系我们。

我们感谢 Hugging Face 全天候响应的团队，对于由此造成的任何干扰，我们深表歉意。 安全永无止境；我们将继续提高标准。

分析人工智能驱动的入侵

该攻击最初是通过人工智能辅助检测发现的。 我们的异常检测管道使用基于 LLM 的安全遥测分类来将真实信号与日常噪声分开，正是这些信号的相关性标记了妥协。

为了了解数以万计的自动化操作群做了什么，我们在完整的攻击者操作日志上运行了 LLM 驱动的分析代理，该日志由 17,000 多个记录的事件组成。 这使我们能够重建时间线，提取妥协指标，映射所涉及的凭证，并将真正的影响与诱饵活动区分开来。 借助这种方法，我们能够在数小时内完成通常需要数天才能完成的任务，并与对手的速度相匹配。

我们可以用于此分析的模型选择受到了我们没有预料到的限制；我们在下面对此进行描述。

不对称问题

当我们开始进行日志分析时，我们首先使用商业API背后的前沿模型。 但这不起作用：分析需要提交大量真实的攻击命令、漏洞利用有效负载和 C2 工件，而这些请求被提供商的安全护栏阻止，无法区分事件响应者和攻击者。 我们在 GLM 5.2（一种开放权重模型）上在我们自己的基础设施上运行了取证分析。 这还有第二个好处：没有攻击者数据，也没有它引用的凭据离开我们的环境。

这一经验指出了一个值得规划的差距。 我们不知道攻击者的代理使用哪种模型，是越狱的托管模型还是不受限制的开放重量模型；无论哪种方式，攻击者都不受使用策略的约束，而我们自己的取证工作则受到我们第一次尝试的托管模型的护栏的阻碍。 防御者的实践教训是：拥有一个可以在事件发生之前在自己的基础设施上运行的强大模型，并进行审查和准备，既可以避免护栏锁定，又可以防止攻击者数据和凭据离开您的环境。 这并不是反对托管模型的安全措施，我们正在与相关提供商分享此反馈。

这意味着什么

自主的、人工智能驱动的进攻工具不再是理论。 它降低了开展广泛、耐心、多阶段活动的成本，并且以机器速度运行。 现在，防御在线平台意味着将数据和模型表面视为一流的攻击表面，并在防御中使用人工智能来跟上步伐。 我们将继续在那里投资，并继续分享我们学到的东西。
